---
title: "Vega-Lite & Vega"
---

Vega-Lite [@satyanarayan2017vegalite] is a grammar of graphics
[@wilkinson2005grammar] in JSON: you declare *what* maps to *what* —
fields to positions, colors, shapes — and layout, scales, axes, and
legends follow. That declarative economy is why it is arguably the most
agent-fluent chart format in existence. Below: a bar chart (@fig-bar), a
multi-series trend (@fig-trend), and a scatter (@fig-scatter).

Raw Vega [@satyanarayan2016reactive] sits one level down and reaches
things Vega-Lite cannot — @fig-net uses its force
transform [@fruchterman1991graph] to lay out a node-edge network with no
coordinates in the source at all. Click to zoom.

![Category totals](figures/chart.vl.json){#fig-bar}

[view source: `chart.vl.json`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/chart.vl.json){.source-link}

![Renders vs cache hits](figures/trend.vl.json){#fig-trend}

[view source: `trend.vl.json`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/trend.vl.json){.source-link}

![Render time by scene size](figures/scatter.vl.json){#fig-scatter}

[view source: `scatter.vl.json`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/scatter.vl.json){.source-link}

![The pipeline as a force-directed graph](figures/network.vg.json){#fig-net}

[view source: `network.vg.json`](https://github.com/seandavi/quarto-livefigures/blob/main/site/gallery/figures/network.vg.json){.source-link}
